text:
British singer-songwriter Adele has stopped mid-concert to tell her fans what she thought of breaker Rachael ‘Raygun’ Gunn's efforts at the Olympics after the Aussie's performance went viral for all the wrong reasons.

Australian breaking athlete Rachael ‘Raygun’ Gunn has drawn the attention of Adele after her performance in Paris became an internet meme, with the English singer telling her 80,000 fans that the dancer was the “best thing that has happened at the Olympics”.

Gunn, who has a PhD in breakdancing and dance culture and lectures at Macquarie University, competed in the first-ever Olympic women’s breakdancing competition on Friday but lost all three pool matches against the USA, France and Lithuania.

The 36-year-old's floor wriggling and kangaroo hops failed to impress the world and the judges, who did not give her a single point.

Gunn is now the victim of intense social media scrutiny, but has found a fan in the renowned British celebrity.

Halting her show at the Munich Messe open-air arena, Adele revealed to her audience of 80,000 fans that she thought the Australian's performance was the “best thing that has happened at the Olympics”.

"I think it's the best thing that has happened at the Olympics for the entire time," the 16-time Grammy Award winner told her fans.

"Now I didn't even know breakdancing was an Olympic sport these days I think that is f***ing fantastic. I really really do."

Gesturing to her crew members on stage with her, the English singer-songwriter told the audience her percussionist was "p***ing himself laughing just thinking about it".

"I can't work out if it was a joke but either way it has made me very very happy and me and my friends have been s***ing ourselves laughing for nearly 24 hours," she said.

"I just wanted to know if you have seen it. If you haven't seen it please leave the show and google it because it is LOLZ.

"It is so f***ing funny and is my favourite thing that has happened in the Olympics this whole whole time."

The 36-year-old athlete is being accused by internet trolls of lying her way into the Olympics and embarrassing her nation, but she is not lacking in support either, with many praising her confidence in tackling something unique.

Breaking head judge Martin Gilian earlier defended the performance, reiterating that the judging panel did not believe her performance was poor, just that her competitors were better.

“I feel personally very sorry,” he said.

“The breaking and hip hop community definitely stands behind her. She was just trying to bring something new, something original and something that represents her country. We stay with her."

"We have five criteria in the comparative judging system. Just her level was maybe not as high as the other competitors," Gilian explained.

“Again, we’re using a comparative judging system. Her competitors were just better but it doesn’t mean that she did really bad. She did her best.”

title: 'I can't work out if it was a joke': Adele stops concert to discuss Raygun's breakdancing performance at Paris Olympic Games | Sky News Australia
description: Australian breaking athlete Rachael ‘Raygun’ Gunn has drawn the attention of Adele after her performance in Paris became an internet meme, with the English singer telling her 80,000 fans that the dancer was the “best thing that has happened at the Olympics”.
